complete. Summary of changes:

## CompatConfig Extended (src/models.rs)
Added 6 new fields to the existing CompatConfig struct:
- `supports_tools` — can disable tool inclusion for providers that don't support them
- `supports_streaming` — flag for streaming capability
- `supports_parallel_tool_calls` — flag for parallel tool call support
- `system_role_name` — override system message role (e.g., 'developer' for o1-series)
- `stop_reason_field` — override stop-reason field name in responses
- `custom_headers` — per-provider HTTP headers injected into every request

Also includes gateway routing metadata: `open_router_routing`, `vercel_gateway_routing`.

## Provider Wiring
- `with_compat()` builder added to all 5 providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenAI Responses, Cohere, Gemini
- `create_provider()` factory wires compat through for all route kinds

## OpenAI Completions Provider (src/providers/openai.rs)
- `system_role_name` overrides system message role (default: 'system')
- `supports_tools=false` omits tools from request even when present
- `max_tokens_field='max_completion_tokens'` routes token limit to the alternative field
- `supports_usage_in_streaming=false` disables include_usage in stream_options
- Custom headers injected between auth headers and per-request StreamOptions headers

## OpenAI Responses Provider (src/providers/openai_responses.rs)
- Custom headers injected in stream() method

## Tests (22 new)
- 9 behavioral tests in openai.rs: system_role_name override, supports_tools true/false, max_tokens routing, usage_in_streaming, combined overrides, custom headers via live TCP server
- 4 CompatConfig deserialization tests in models.rs: all fields, default, empty object
- 9 factory propagation tests already existed in mod.rs (from earlier in this session)

All 142 provider tests pass. Clean clippy.","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:17:26Z"},{"id":953,"issue_id":"bd-3uqg.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: compat override mechanism wired to all 5 provider implementations (Anthropic, OpenAI Completions, OpenAI Responses, Cohere, Gemini). (2) Injected custom_headers from compat config in all provider stream() methods with correct priority (compat < per-request StreamOptions). (3) Updated create_provider() factory to pass entry.compat.clone() for all 10 route kinds. (4) 11 new tests: 7 factory compat-propagation tests + 2 HTTP-level header injection tests + 2 None-compat regression guards. All 100 provider+model tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:19:32Z"}]}

Created tests/provider_backward_lock.rs with 132 lock tests.

## Changes Made

### Source: Made build_request/streaming_url public
- `src/providers/anthropic.rs`: `build_request` → `pub`, `AnthropicRequest` → `pub struct`
- `src/providers/openai.rs`: `build_request` → `pub`, `OpenAIRequest` → `pub struct`
- `src/providers/openai_responses.rs`: `build_request` → `pub`, `OpenAIResponsesRequest` → `pub struct`
- `src/providers/cohere.rs`: `build_request` → `pub`, `CohereRequest` → `pub struct`
- `src/providers/gemini.rs`: `build_request` + `streaming_url` → `pub`, `GeminiRequest` → `pub struct`

### Tests: tests/provider_backward_lock.rs (132 tests)

**Per-provider request shape lock** (~7 tests each):
- Anthropic: request shape, default max_tokens=8192, tool shape (input_schema), thinking config, thinking budget auto-bump, Off omits field, provider identity
- OpenAI Completions: request shape (system as first message), default max_tokens=4096, tool shape (function wrapper), compat overrides (max_completion_tokens, developer role, tools disabled, usage streaming disabled), provider identity, provider name override
- OpenAI Responses: request shape (input array), default max_output_tokens=4096, tool shape (flat), provider identity
- Cohere: request shape (v2 messages), default max_tokens=4096, tool shape, provider identity
- Gemini: request shape (systemInstruction, camelCase), default maxOutputTokens=8192, tool shape (functionDeclarations), toolConfig AUTO mode, no toolConfig without tools, streaming URL shape, provider identity

**Factory routing lock** (6 tests):
- Routes Anthropic, OpenAI Completions, OpenAI Responses, Cohere, Google correctly
- Batch A1 OAI-compatible providers route through factory

**Cross-provider invariant lock** (3 tests):
- system_prompt_handling_differs_by_provider: top-level string vs messages[0] vs systemInstruction
- max_tokens_field_name_differs_by_provider: max_tokens vs max_output_tokens vs generationConfig.maxOutputTokens
- tool_nesting_shape_differs_by_provider: flat vs function wrapper vs flat+type vs functionDeclarations

**Thinking budget lock** (2 tests):
- All 5 budget levels locked: Minimal=1024, Low=2048, Medium=8192, High=16384, XHigh=32768
- Custom budget overrides honored

All 132 tests pass. Clean clippy.","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:32:26Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-7rmt","title":"Conformance report archival and regression tracking","description":"# Conformance report archival and regression tracking

## What
Each conformance run produces a JSON report. Archive these reports and track conformance over time. Alert on regressions (pass rate drops).

## Implementation
1. After each full conformance run, save report to reports/ directory with timestamp
2. Compare current report to previous report
3. If pass rate decreased OR new failures appeared: flag regression
4. Generate trend chart: pass rate over time

## Regression Detection
- Compare extension-by-extension: any extension that was PASS and is now FAIL = regression
- Compare aggregate: overall pass rate must be >= previous run
- New extensions added (not in previous run) do not count as regressions

## Output
- reports/conformance_YYYY-MM-DD.json (archived per run)
- reports/conformance_trend.json (historical trend data)
- CI annotation on regression

## Acceptance Criteria
- Regression detected when pass rate drops
- Historical reports accessible
- Trend data shows improvement over time","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:54.722091165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:57:36.185965699Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:57:36.185897943Z","close_reason":"Archived conformance reports + trend output + regression detection/CI annotations","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7rmt","depends_on_id":"bd-19rt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:54.728950744Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-8mm","title":"Impl: Deterministic event loop scheduler (asupersync)","description":"# Goal
Implement the **deterministic PiJS event loop** on top of asupersync, matching the spec in `bd-123`.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Queue model: microtasks, timers, hostcall completions.
- Timer wheel or heap with stable ordering guarantees.
- Hostcall completion enqueue with stable tie-breaking.
- Single-threaded scheduler loop that is reproducible under fixed inputs.
- Trace IDs for scheduled items (for deterministic logs and replay).

# Determinism Proof Obligations
- Ordering preserved: microtasks always drain before timers.
- Tie-break: FIFO by enqueue time and sequence id.
- No hidden randomness or time-based ordering.

# Tests
- Unit tests for queue ordering and tie-breaks.
- Conformance fixtures for microtask/timer ordering.
- E2E trace replay via harness (bd-2dd).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
